t: The War of the Ring in Twelve Stops
s: September 3018 to November 3019 — the whole war, told across every hall of the archive

stops.n: Begin where the Tale of Years quickens to days: the war's spine on one axis, from the June assault on Osgiliath to the Field of Cormallen.
September 23: watch the golden thread begin. Press ride and the whole year unspools — every marker moves on the day App B gives it.

stops.t: At the sign of the Prancing Pony
stops.n: Bree, where the Road meets the Road — and a letter waits, and a man called Strider rises from the shadows by the wall.
Weathertop: the old watchtower of Amon Sûl, razed thrice over three thousand years, and the Morgul-blade of October 6.

stops.t: The first battle of the war
stops.n: The Fords of Isen — where Théodred fell in February so that Rohan would be awake in March. The counters move as the corpus tells it.

stops.t: The deep breath before the plunge
stops.n: The Hornburg, March 3-4: ten thousand at the wall, the horn of Helm at dawn, and a forest that was not there the night before.
The Pelennor Fields entire — the Rammas breached, the Rohirrim at sunrise, the black sails that turned out to be hope. Scrub the phases.

stops.t: Two hobbits crossing a dead land
stops.n: Set the day-bar to March: Frodo and Sam's orange thread crawls from the Morannon south while every army in the West rides the other way.
The Morannon, March 25: six thousand against ten times their number — a battle fought to buy two hobbits three more steps.
Orodruin, where the thing was made and could be unmade. The record gathers everything the archive knows of it.

stops.t: The war ends where it began
stops.n: Bywater, November 3: three hundred hobbits, one barricade, and the Shire scoured by its own people.
September 29, 3021: the Third Age ends aboard a white ship. The timeline's last flags — and the Fourth Age beyond them.

t: One Ring of Green Stones: the Ring of Barahir
s: A single jewel handed down through every Age — the archive's longest unbroken thread

stops.n: Start at the treasure itself: twin serpents, eyes of emerald — Finrod's ring of Valinor, and the chain of custody that never quite breaks.
Finrod Felagund, who swore friendship to Bëor's house and died in the dark of Tol-in-Gaurhoth keeping the oath the ring embodied.

stops.t: The tower where the oath was paid
stops.n: Tol Sirion — Finrod's isle, Sauron's werewolf-keep, the pits where a king of Nargothrond wrestled for a mortal man.
Silmariën of Númenor, in whose line the ring waited out an Age — through the Lords of Andúnië to the ships of Elendil.
T.A. 1975: Arvedui Last-king drowns in the Ice-bay, but the ring is already with the Lossoth — ransomed back, alone of the North's treasures.
Aragorn — who gave the heirloom of forty generations to Arwen on Cerin Amroth. The record assembles the whole life around that gift.

t: The Rise and Drowning of Númenor
s: From a grateful gift to a bent world, in seven stops

stops.n: The taxonomy shows what the Edain became: thrice-lengthened lives, late marriage, few children — glory with a demographic doom built in.
Elros Tar-Minyatur, who chose mortality and got five hundred years of it — the whole royal line hangs from this one choice.
S.A. 1700: Tar-Minastir's navy takes Sauron front and rear at the Gwathló — the corpus's one great amphibious campaign, animated.
Watch the flags cluster: the Shadow on the island, the King's Men, Sauron taken to Armenelos 'as a hostage' — and the fleets grow.
The palantíri and Nimloth's fruit — the little that came out of the wreck in nine ships, and became two kingdoms.
The population chart's most terrible line: Tolkien's own curve for the island, ending in a vertical fall at 3319.
Elendil the Tall — and everything after him: the archive's whole Third Age hangs from this node.

t: The Three Roads of the Silmarils
s: Heaven, earth and sea — the jewels that made the First Age, in six stops

stops.n: Y.T. 1449-50: the timeline's Elder-Days band, where three jewels and one oath set everything after in motion.
The Gallery's plates of the Elder Days — the panoplies of Gondolin and Doriath that armed the wars the jewels caused.
The Nirnaeth animated: the bait, the trap, Turgon's ten thousand, Húrin's stand — the war for the jewels at its most terrible.
The Silmarils' own record: crown to Beren's hand to Nauglamír to the sky — and the last two to the chasm and the wave.
Eärendil the Half-elven, who carried a jewel to Valinor and became a star — the node where all three kindreds' lines meet.
F.A. c.542: the Star rises over a drowning Beleriand. The timeline shows what it cost to put it there.

t: A Walking Tour of Drowned Beleriand
s: The lands under the waves, as the map remembers them

stops.n: Slide the age-bar to the Years of the Trees: Beleriand alive — Doriath fenced, the Falas bright, Angband biding.
The sea-blue thread: Mithrim to the Hidden City in thirty-seven days, past the ruins of a world already ending.
Menegroth — fairest dwelling of any king east of the Sea, sacked twice, mourned always.
Nargothrond behind its river-gorge: stealth-war doctrine, one fatal bridge.

stops.t: The fall of the Hidden City
stops.n: Gondolin's siege animated — over the northern heights at the Gates of Summer, and the long escape by the Eagles' cleft.
The Hall of Verse holds the Lay of Leithian's opening lines — the sound of Beleriand, outlasting its geography.
Slide forward one age: the blue overlay takes it all. Only Himling's hilltop and a rim of Lindon remain — and the archive proved they match.

s: From the Deathless to the Last, through fire and gold

stops.n: The taxonomy's dwarf-card: a third women at most, single marriages, mansion-vendettas — why every loss is nearly permanent.
Durin I, eldest of the Seven Fathers — and the line that keeps returning to his name, drawn whole to Durin VII the Last.
Khazad-dûm's West-gate: ithildin, a riddle in Sindarin, and everything the archive knows about the greatest dwelling of the Age.
Azanulbizar, T.A. 2799: the whole line's grief spent in one valley — 'barely half could still stand'. Animated, phase by phase.
Erebor — Thráin's Arkenstone, Smaug's bed, Dáin's kingship, a siege withstood in the War.
The battle that ended the wandering days — goblins and wargs against elves, men and Durin's folk, and the Eagles at the last.
The Gallery's Erebor plate: knee-length mail, mattock and round shield — the museum of a people who forge their own history.
